# SPICE Syntax Cheatsheet (LTspice flavor)

A quick reference for writing `.cir` files that LTspice will actually open and simulate. Skim this before emitting a netlist by hand; better, use `scripts/write_cir.py` which enforces these rules.

---

## File structure

```
* <Title comment — must be first line if no .TITLE card>
* Generated by Claude LTspice_simulation skill on YYYY-MM-DD

* --- Sources ---
V1 VCC 0 9
V2 IN  0 SINE(0 0.1 1k)

* --- Components ---
R1 IN  N001 1k
C1 N001 OUT 1u
...

* --- Models / Subcircuits ---
.model MyDiode D(Is=1e-14 N=1)
.include opamp_lib.sub   ; only if unavoidable — prefer embedding

* --- Analysis ---
.ac dec 100 1 1Meg
.op

* --- Save directives ---
.save V(IN) V(OUT) V(N001)

.end
```

Rules that matter:

- **First line is always a comment.** LTspice treats it as the title. If your first line is a component card instead, you'll get a cryptic error.
- **`.end` is mandatory.** Missing it → LTspice silently ignores everything after the last directive.
- **One statement per line.** No semicolon-separated compound lines.
- **Comments start with `*` (full-line) or `;` (end-of-line).**
- **Continuation lines start with `+`** in column 1.

---

## Device cards — pin order matters

| Letter | Device | Pin order |
|---|---|---|
| R | Resistor | `R<name> n1 n2 <value>` |
| C | Capacitor | `C<name> n1 n2 <value> [ic=<V0>]` |
| L | Inductor | `L<name> n1 n2 <value> [ic=<I0>]` |
| D | Diode | `D<name> anode cathode <model>` |
| Q | BJT | `Q<name> C B E [S] <model>` |
| M | MOSFET | `M<name> D G S B <model>` (bulk usually = source) |
| J | JFET | `J<name> D G S <model>` |
| V | Voltage source | `V<name> n+ n- <spec>` |
| I | Current source | `I<name> n+ n- <spec>` |
| X | Subcircuit call | `X<name> <pins in subckt order> <subckt_name> [params]` |
| E | VCVS | `E<name> n+ n- nc+ nc- <gain>` |
| G | VCCS | `G<name> n+ n- nc+ nc- <gm>` |
| H | CCVS | `H<name> n+ n- <vsense> <transres>` |
| F | CCCS | `F<name> n+ n- <vsense> <gain>` |

Wrong pin order on Q/M/D is a silent killer — the simulation runs but gives nonsense.

---

## Source specs

```
V1 IN 0 DC 5                              ; plain DC
V1 IN 0 AC 1                              ; unit AC stimulus for .ac
V1 IN 0 SINE(offset amp freq [Td Theta Phi Ncycles])
V1 IN 0 PULSE(V1 V2 Td Tr Tf Ton Tper [Ncycles])
V1 IN 0 PWL(t1 v1 t2 v2 t3 v3 …)
V1 IN 0 EXP(V1 V2 Td1 Tau1 Td2 Tau2)
V1 IN 0 SFFM(Voff Vamp Fcar MDI Fsig)
```

For an AC-coupled amplifier you usually want `AC 1` for the frequency sweep AND a `SINE(…)` source gated by `.tran` — you can combine: `V1 IN 0 SINE(0 0.1 1k) AC 1`.

---

## Analysis directives

```
.op                                           ; DC operating point
.dc <source> <start> <stop> <step>            ; DC sweep
.ac dec <N> <fstart> <fstop>                  ; AC sweep, N points/decade
.ac oct <N> <fstart> <fstop>
.ac lin <N> <fstart> <fstop>
.tran <Tstep> <Tstop> [Tstart [Tmaxstep]]     ; transient
.tran 0 <Tstop> 0 <Tmaxstep>                  ; most common form
.noise V(out) <input_src> dec <N> <fstart> <fstop>
.tf V(out) <input_src>                        ; DC transfer function / gain
```

Typical sensible defaults:
- `.ac dec 100 1 10Meg` for general audio/RF small-signal.
- `.tran 0 10m 0 1u` for audio-band transient.
- `.tran 0 <10*Tperiod> 0 <Tperiod/1000>` for oscillators/switchers.

---

## Options worth knowing

```
.options gmin=1e-10 abstol=1e-9 reltol=1e-3   ; loosen tolerance for convergence
.options method=gear                           ; sometimes helps stiff circuits
.options plotwinsize=0                         ; disables waveform compression, big data files
.options temp=27                               ; simulation temperature
.param <name>=<value>                          ; parameter for .step
.step param <name> <start> <stop> <step>
```

---

## Engineering notation

LTspice accepts these suffixes (case-insensitive except where noted):

| Suffix | Multiplier |
|---|---|
| `T` | 1e12 |
| `G` | 1e9 |
| `Meg` | 1e6  ← **must be `Meg`, NOT `M`** |
| `K` | 1e3 |
| `M` | 1e-3 ← **milli, NOT mega** |
| `u` | 1e-6 |
| `n` | 1e-9 |
| `p` | 1e-12 |
| `f` | 1e-15 |

Never use unicode (`µ`, `Ω`). Never append units (`uF`, `ohm`, `Hz`).

The `M` vs `Meg` collision is the #1 bug in hand-written SPICE. 1M means 1 milli. 1Meg means 1 million.

---

## Node naming

- `0` and `GND` both refer to ground (node 0). Most schematics use `0` in the netlist.
- Every other node is a user-chosen name. Stick to `[A-Za-z0-9_]`.
- Every node must appear on at least two components — a dangling net is an error.
- Labels are case-insensitive but the waveform viewer preserves case.

---

## Common gotchas

1. **`V1 N1 N2 1k` is wrong** — a 1k voltage source is `V1 N1 N2 1000` or `V1 N1 N2 1k` interpreted as 1000 volts, not ohms. Don't write units on sources.
2. **`.ac` without any AC source gives a flat line.** At least one source must have `AC 1` (or another amplitude) in its spec.
3. **`.tran` without a time-varying source gives a flat line.** Need a SINE/PULSE/PWL/EXP source or a non-zero initial condition.
4. **Convergence failures on switchers:** soft-start the input (Tr=1m on the V source), loosen `.options gmin abstol`, reduce maxstep.
5. **Op-amp rails missing:** universal op-amp subcircuits need V+ and V- connected to real rails, not left floating.
6. **Electrolytic polarity is not enforced in SPICE** but is enforced in LTspice's default component library if the user opens the `.asc`. When writing `.cir` directly, just get the polarity right in the netlist and comment it.
